description:
A backup engineer has asked you to confirm the integrity of the latest backup file that sits in their
    data directory.

    1. Location of the file that must be checked:
       /home/user/data/backup.tar.gz

    2. Directory where the integrity report must be stored:
       /home/user/reports

    3. What you must do:
       • Calculate the SHA-256 checksum of /home/user/data/backup.tar.gz.  
       • Write the result to a **new** file called  
         /home/user/reports/backup_integrity.log

    4. Required output format INSIDE /home/user/reports/backup_integrity.log  
       (this exact structure will be graded by an automated test):
       • One single line only.  
       • The line contains exactly two whitespace characters between the fields (two spaces, not one,
         not a tab).  
       • Field 1: the 64-character hexadecimal SHA-256 digest in lowercase.  
       • Field 2: the literal filename string “backup.tar.gz”.  
       • A single trailing newline character **must** finish the line.

       Consequently, the file’s sole line must look like:
       ‹64_hex_chars›␠␠backup.tar.gz␤  
       (where ␠ represents one space; you must have two of them).

    5. Constraints:
       • Achieve the entire task with a single compound shell command executed from the terminal.  
       • Do not manually type the checksum; it must be produced by a checksum utility.  
       • Do not modify the backup file.  
       • Do not create additional files or directories.

    When the task is complete, the file /home/user/reports/backup_integrity.log should exist and match
    the exact format above.  The test harness will fail if the spacing, filename, number of lines,
    capitalisation, or newline handling deviates.
truth:
Pre-existing state before the agent starts:

    1. Directory & file structure:
       /home/user/data/
           backup.tar.gz    (ASCII file, five bytes, content: “hello” with **no** trailing newline)
       /home/user/reports/  (directory, initially empty)

    2. SHA-256 digest that the automated test expects inside the log:
       2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824

    3. Correct final content of /home/user/reports/backup_integrity.log:
       “2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824  backup.tar.gz\n”

       (That is: the digest above, two space characters, the literal text “backup.tar.gz”, and a single
       newline character.)
name: task_000000_3cd03a5d
